164	// ── luaT_init ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
165	
166	// C: void luaT_init (lua_State *L)
167	/// Intern all metamethod name strings and pin them in the GC.
168	///
169	/// Must be called exactly once during `LuaState` initialization, before any
170	/// metamethod lookup.  After this call, `GlobalState.tmname[i]` holds the
171	/// interned `LuaString` for metamethod `i`.
172	pub(crate) fn init(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
173	    // C: static const char *const luaT_eventname[] = {  /* ORDER TM */
174	    //     "__index", "__newindex",
175	    //     "__gc", "__mode", "__len", "__eq",
176	    //     "__add", "__sub", "__mul", "__mod", "__pow",
177	    //     "__div", "__idiv",
178	    //     "__band", "__bor", "__bxor", "__shl", "__shr",
179	    //     "__unm", "__bnot", "__lt", "__le",
180	    //     "__concat", "__call", "__close"
181	    // };
182	    const EVENT_NAMES: &[&[u8]] = &[
183	        b"__index",
184	        b"__newindex",
185	        b"__gc",
186	        b"__mode",
187	        b"__len",
188	        b"__eq",
189	        b"__add",
190	        b"__sub",
191	        b"__mul",
192	        b"__mod",
193	        b"__pow",
194	        b"__div",
195	        b"__idiv",
196	        b"__band",
197	        b"__bor",
198	        b"__bxor",
199	        b"__shl",
200	        b"__shr",
201	        b"__unm",
202	        b"__bnot",
203	        b"__lt",
204	        b"__le",
205	        b"__concat",
206	        b"__call",
207	        b"__close",
208	    ];
209	    debug_assert!(EVENT_NAMES.len() == TM_N);
210	
211	    // C: for (i=0; i<TM_N; i++) {
212	    //     G(L)->tmname[i] = luaS_new(L, luaT_eventname[i]);
213	    //     luaC_fix(L, obj2gco(G(L)->tmname[i]));  /* never collect these names */
214	    // }
215	    for (i, &name) in EVENT_NAMES.iter().enumerate() {
216	        // C: luaS_new(L, luaT_eventname[i])
217	        let interned = state.intern_str(name)?;
218	        // C: G(L)->tmname[i] = ...
219	        state.global_mut().tmname[i] = interned.clone();
220	        // C: luaC_fix(L, obj2gco(...))
221	        // Pin the string so the GC never collects it.
222	        // TODO(port): luaC_fix API on gc() is TBD; no-op in Phase A–C (Rc keeps it alive)
223	        state.gc().fix_object(&interned);
224	    }
225	    Ok(())
226	}

261	        // C: else return tm;

delta.text:
Now I understand the bug. Two fixes needed:

1. `tagmethods::

init` is defined but never called from `lua_open()`.
2. `tmname` Vec is initialized empty, so even when `init` is called it would index

-out-of-bounds.

Let me fix both.

content.text:
Now I understand the bug. Two fixes needed:

1. `tagmethods::init` is defined but never called from `lua_open()`.
2. `tmname` Vec is initialized empty, so even when `init` is called it would index-out-of-bounds.

Let me fix both.

## Report
**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/tagmeth
ods.rs:304` panicked with index OOB because `global_State
.tmname` was an empty `Vec`. Two related

bugs in initialization:
1. `crate::tagmethods::init(state)` was left as a T
ODO in `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:2210` (the C `luaT_init` call site) and never invoked, so the metamethod name str

ings were never populated.
2. `tmname: Vec::new()` in `state.rs:2666` started
empty, so even when `tagmethods::init` ran, the indexed assignment `tmname[i] = ...` would also

OOB.

**The fix:**
- `state.rs:2210` — replaced the TODO st
ub with the real call `crate::tagmethods::init(state)?;` inside `lua_open()`, restoring the C `

luaT_init(L)` sequence.
- `tagmethods.rs:init` — before the indexed-assignment loop, resize `tmname` to `TM_
N` entries using a placeholder interned string. This bridges the empty-Vec construction in
`lua_state_init` with the fixed-array contract `tmname[event

]` access assumes.

**New state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (no errors, only pre-existing warnings).
Did not re-run the test program per instructions — the mega-loop will re-test.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/tagmethods.rs:304` panicked with index OOB because `global_State.tmname` was an empty `Vec`. Two related bugs in initialization:
1. `crate::tagmethods::init(state)` was left as a TODO in `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:2210` (the C `luaT_init` call site) and never invoked, so the metamethod name strings were never populated.
2. `tmname: Vec::new()` in `state.rs:2666` started empty, so even when `tagmethods::init` ran, the indexed assignment `tmname[i] = ...` would also OOB.

**The fix:**
- `state.rs:2210` — replaced the TODO stub with the real call `crate::tagmethods::init(state)?;` inside `lua_open()`, restoring the C `luaT_init(L)` sequence.
- `tagmethods.rs:init` — before the indexed-assignment loop, resize `tmname` to `TM_N` entries using a placeholder interned string. This bridges the empty-Vec construction in `lua_state_init` with the fixed-array contract `tmname[event]` access assumes.

**New state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (no errors, only pre-existing warnings). Did not re-run the test program per instructions — the mega-loop will re-test.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/tagmethods.rs:304` panicked with index OOB because `global_State.tmname` was an empty `Vec`. Two related bugs in initialization:
1. `crate::tagmethods::init(state)` was left as a TODO in `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:2210` (the C `luaT_init` call site) and never invoked, so the metamethod name strings were never populated.
2. `tmname: Vec::new()` in `state.rs:2666` started empty, so even when `tagmethods::init` ran, the indexed assignment `tmname[i] = ...` would also OOB.

**The fix:**
- `state.rs:2210` — replaced the TODO stub with the real call `crate::tagmethods::init(state)?;` inside `lua_open()`, restoring the C `luaT_init(L)` sequence.
- `tagmethods.rs:init` — before the indexed-assignment loop, resize `tmname` to `TM_N` entries using a placeholder interned string. This bridges the empty-Vec construction in `lua_state_init` with the fixed-array contract `tmname[event]` access assumes.

**New state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (no errors, only pre-existing warnings). Did not re-run the test program per instructions — the mega-loop will re-test.
